title: Join the Children’s Commissioner for England Youth Advisory Board on Artificial Intelligence in Education 2026–2027
summary: Help Shape the Future of Artificial Intelligence in Education Across England The Children’s Commissioner for England is inviting passionate and forward-thinking young people to become members of a newly established Youth Advisory Board focused on Artificial Intelligence (AI) in Education. This exciting opportunity gives young people a direct platform to share their experiences, perspectives, and ideas about how AI is influencing learning, teaching, and education across England. As AI technologies continue to transform classrooms, learning tools, and educational experiences, it is increasingly important that young people have a voice in the conversations that shape future policies. The Youth Advisory Board has been created to ensure that policymakers hear directly from the young people who are most affected by these changes. Through regular meetings, discussions, and collaborative engagement with policymakers, board members will play a meaningful role in helping the Department for Education understand the realities of young people s experiences with AI and contribute to recommendations that could influence educational policy for years to come. About the Youth Advisory Board The Youth Advisory Board is a group of young people brought together to provide valuable insights into the opportunities and challenges associated with Artificial Intelligence in education. Members of the board will have the opportunity to: Share their personal experiences of using AI in educational settings. Discuss how AI is affecting learning, teaching, assessment, and student wellbeing. Highlight concerns, opportunities, and recommendations regarding AI in schools and colleges. Contribute to discussions that may influence future educational policies. Ensure that the voices of young people remain at the center of decisions about educational technology. The initiative recognizes that young people are among the most active users of emerging technologies and therefore have unique insights that can help shape effective and responsible AI policies. Benefits of Joining the Youth Advisory Board Successful participants will enjoy several valuable benefits, including: 1. Influence National Education Policy Members will have a direct opportunity to inform discussions and policy recommendations related to Artificial Intelligence in Education. Their experiences and opinions will help policymakers better understand how AI is impacting young people. 2. Develop Leadership Skills Participation in the board will help members strengthen essential skills such as: Public speaking Communication Critical thinking Policy engagement Teamwork Problem-solving Advocacy 3. Collaborate with Other Young Leaders Participants will join a diverse group of young people from across England who are passionate about education, technology, and creating positive change. 4. Gain Exposure to Emerging Technologies Board members will deepen their understanding of Artificial Intelligence and its growing role within education systems. 5. Make a Lasting Impact The recommendations and insights generated by the board may contribute to future decisions that affect children and young people throughout England. Meeting Structure and Commitment Successful applicants should be prepared to actively participate throughout the duration of the program. Key Commitments Include: Introductory In-Person Meeting Attend an introductory face-to-face meeting in London during July 2026 (exact date to be confirmed). Regular Advisory Board Meetings Meet every six weeks with fellow advisory board members. Participate in meetings until December 2027. Hybrid Participation Format The advisory board will use a combination of in-person and online engagement: Four meetings will be held in person. Remaining meetings will be conducted online. This structure ensures meaningful collaboration while allowing flexibility for participants from different parts of England. Why Artificial Intelligence in Education Matters Artificial Intelligence is rapidly becoming part of everyday educational experiences. Students increasingly encounter AI-powered learning platforms, educational assistants, assessment tools, and digital learning resources. The Children’s Commissioner for England recognizes that young people are uniquely positioned to provide feedback on: How AI supports learning. Potential risks and challenges associated with AI use. Fairness and accessibility concerns. Privacy and safety considerations. The future role of technology in education. The Youth Advisory Board provides a valuable opportunity for young people to contribute to these important discussions and ensure that educational policies reflect real-world experiences. Eligibility Requirements To be eligible for the Youth Advisory Board, applicants must: Live in England. Be between 16 and 18 years old in July 2026. Be willing to participate in both online and in-person meetings. Have an interest in discussing Artificial Intelligence and its role in education. Be prepared to contribute their views and experiences throughout the advisory period. Deadline No specific application deadline has been announced. Interested applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible once applications are available. APPLY HERE Final Thoughts The Children’s Commissioner for England Youth Advisory Board on Artificial Intelligence in Education offers an exceptional opportunity for young people to influence national conversations about the future of education. By sharing their experiences, insights, and recommendations, participants can help ensure that Artificial Intelligence develops in ways that genuinely benefit children and young people across England. Young people who are passionate about technology, education, and creating positive change should consider applying and becoming part of this important initiative. For more information about this opportunity, visit here . Discover more Global Fellowship Opportunities on OFY here .
